title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.
writeup: The video demonstrates free-claude-code, an open-source proxy by Ali Sherer that intercepts Claude Code's API traffic and re-routes it to cheaper backend models while preserving the full Claude Code terminal interface and commands. The presenter builds a habit tracker app called Habitual for roughly $0.03 using DeepSeek V4 Flash via OpenRouter, compared to an estimated $5-10 on Anthropic credits, a ratio consistent with the claimed 2-5% cost floor. Three backend options are covered: OpenRouter, Nvidia NIM, and Ollama (local GPU). The core argument is that 80-90% of Opus 4.7 quality is achievable at a fraction of the price, making a hybrid frontier-plus-cheap model strategy practical for high-volume coding work.
key_points: The free-claude-code proxy runs as a local server (default port 8082) that transparently intercepts Claude Code requests before they reach Anthropic, substituting a cheaper backend model while the developer sees no change in the CLI interface, commands, or thinking-block output.

DeepSeek V4 Flash via OpenRouter costs approximately $0.14 per million tokens versus $25 per million for Claude Opus, which allowed the presenter to build a complete habit tracker app for $0.03 where the equivalent Anthropic run would have cost $5-10.

Three supported backends span different cost and infrastructure tradeoffs: OpenRouter provides the widest paid model selection at low per-token rates, Nvidia NIM is free with an account and runs on Nvidia GPU infrastructure, and Ollama runs models locally on the user's own GPU with no per-token cost and potentially faster throughput when uncongested.

Setup requires three terminal commands to install dependencies and clone the repo, followed by editing the hidden .env file to insert the provider API key and the target model name. On macOS the hidden file is reached via Command-Shift-Period to reveal dotfiles in Finder.

A practical hybrid strategy emerges from the cost analysis: route high-stakes architectural and creative decisions through a frontier model such as Opus 4.7, and handle heavy refactoring, boilerplate, and mechanical edits through a cheap model via the proxy, targeting a 5-10x reduction in overall spend without sacrificing output quality on the decisions that matter most.
